source: Barry Schwartz: The Paradox of Choice (TED 2005)
segment: Two negative effects of choice: paralysis and opportunity costs/regret
note: Audio file must be manually extracted from the TED talk. Download from ted.com or TEDLIUM-3 dataset.

questions.question: What is the main argument of this segment?
options: More choices lead to better decisions
Having too many options has negative psychological consequences
People should avoid making any choices
Choice is only problematic for indecisive people

questions.question: According to the speaker, what are the two main negative effects of too much choice?
options: Confusion and anger
Paralysis and dissatisfaction
Fatigue and boredom
Anxiety and depression

questions.question: What example does the speaker give about retirement savings?
options: Employees invest too aggressively when given many options
Participation rates decrease when more fund options are offered
Most employees choose the safest fund regardless of options
Employers should not offer retirement plans

questions.question: What happened when the speaker went to buy jeans?
options: He couldn't find his size
He had to choose among many fits and styles
The store was out of stock
He decided to buy online instead

questions.question: According to the Vanguard study, for every 10 additional funds offered, participation dropped by approximately how much?
options: 1 percent
2 percent
5 percent
10 percent

questions.question: Why does the speaker suggest that people might feel worse even when they make objectively better choices?
options: Because better choices are more expensive
Because comparing with alternatives creates regret about what was given up
Because people cannot recognize quality
Because society pressures people to be unhappy

questions.question: What can be inferred about the speaker's view of freedom of choice?
options: It should be eliminated entirely
It is beneficial only up to a point, after which it becomes harmful
It is always positive regardless of the number of options
It is only problematic in consumer decisions

questions.question: Why does the speaker mention the concept of 'opportunity costs'?
options: To explain why products are becoming more expensive
To show how awareness of rejected alternatives reduces satisfaction with chosen ones
To argue that people should research every option before deciding
To demonstrate that choices have no real consequences

questions.question: When the speaker says he was 'paralyzed' by the choice of jeans, he most likely means he:
options: Was physically unable to move
Found it extremely difficult to make a decision
Was frightened by the store layout
Could not afford the jeans

questions.question: How would you describe the speaker's overall tone in this segment?
options: Angry and confrontational
Informative and gently provocative
Sad and pessimistic
Casual and indifferent
